title: Lisa Goes Gaga

jokes.text: Birthplace of the brass knuckle, the Nigerian Prince scam, and putting a tomato on your grilled cheese sandwich.
jokes.setup: Springfield's dubious claims to fame, building expectation of weird accomplishments
jokes.punchline: The mundane tomato-on-grilled-cheese as equal to criminal innovations
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons escalation that ends on the absurdly mundane. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: Aw, he's a dumb kid, but he's an above-average dog. Roll over, son. Yay! Aw, nobody saw.
jokes.setup: Father treating his child like a dog
jokes.punchline: The disappointing 'nobody saw' when Ralph successfully performs the trick
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Ralph and his father. The anticlimax of 'nobody saw' adds extra layer.

jokes.text: Then the Mormons got a heads-up.
jokes.setup: Mystical sequence of creatures sensing something coming
jokes.punchline: Mormons lumped in with animals as having supernatural detection abilities
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical joke about Mormon cultural perception. Quick and effective.

jokes.text: Horses are spooked, cows ain't milkin', and look what the chickens laid. Sparkle eggs?!
jokes.setup: Traditional omens of supernatural disturbance
jokes.punchline: Chickens laying glittery eggs instead of normal ones
jokes.explanation: Good visual escalation building to Lady Gaga's arrival. The sparkle eggs are a nice touch.

jokes.text: Conductor, you know the rules. Take off some clothes. Oh, uh, yes, ma'am. sorry, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga establishing her eccentric authority
jokes.punchline: Casual demand for conductor to undress as routine protocol
jokes.explanation: Establishes Gaga's character through absurd but matter-of-fact demand. Well-integrated.

jokes.text: has the lowest self-esteem of any place on the planet
jokes.setup: Describing Springfield's characteristics
jokes.punchline: The statistical impossibility of measuring collective self-esteem
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's theme efficiently. Good economy but fairly standard observational humor.

jokes.text: Don't you remember what happened at LaGuardia? Ready. Yeah, well, uh, you missed your flight. Then we'll party on the boarding ramp!
jokes.setup: Expected disaster story from LaGuardia
jokes.punchline: The 'disaster' was just missing a flight and partying instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that characterizes Gaga's positive attitude. Well-structured payoff.

jokes.text: We spend 90 minutes training 'em and off they go.
jokes.setup: Assistant dramatically quits
jokes.punchline: Gaga treats it like routine turnover with minimal training investment
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and deadpan delivery. Shows Gaga's nonchalant attitude toward drama.

jokes.text: I just can't ignore a billboard in pain.
jokes.setup: Gaga's reason for stopping in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing a billboard as suffering
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd and economical. Perfectly captures Gaga's dramatic empathy. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Campus Clown. Bart Simpson. That's my brother. Four years out of four.
jokes.setup: School popularity awards ceremony
jokes.punchline: Bart's consistent winning of an inherently unflattering award
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. The 'four years out of four' adds nice specificity.

jokes.text: Prank you! Prank you very much!
jokes.setup: Bart accepting his Campus Clown award
jokes.punchline: Elvis impression merged with prank terminology
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that fits Bart's character. Economy is good but pun is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Call back.
jokes.setup: Bart's theatrical acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: Skinner's dry stage direction comment
jokes.explanation: Subtle meta joke - Skinner treating this like a performance. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: What?! Without awards shows, how would I know what movie has the best beheading, or that Glee is a comedy?
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning the value of awards shows
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd examples of awards show 'utility'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of both awards shows and Glee. Good structure with escalating absurdity.

jokes.text: She actually accepted it!
jokes.setup: Lisa accepting the 'Least Popular Student' award
jokes.punchline: Students' surprise that she went through with the humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that amplifies Lisa's humiliation. Solid reaction beat.

jokes.text: You said they'd take it easy on me. I know very little about children.
jokes.setup: Lisa's complaint about bad advice
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' casual admission of incompetence with children
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and character moment. Chalmers' honesty is both shocking and funny.

jokes.text: Burn it. Burn it now.
jokes.setup: Assistant mentions Jennifer Lopez wore the same dress
jokes.punchline: Gaga's immediate, dramatic demand to destroy the dress
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gaga's dramatic nature. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I've been on here since Chicago! I just want my life back!
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga's extravagant train
jokes.punchline: Passenger trapped unwillingly on the party train
jokes.explanation: Nice background gag that adds to the absurdity of Gaga's world. Good economy.

jokes.text: I'm missing a terrible winter!
jokes.setup: Passenger complaining about being trapped
jokes.punchline: Missing something unpleasant as a source of complaint
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic twist on what someone would miss. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: And that's how Berber carpet got its name according to some.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently telling a story
jokes.punchline: Complete non-sequitur about carpet etymology with qualifier
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur - the 'according to some' hedge makes it funnier by suggesting dubious authority.

jokes.text: I thought she knew.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Lisa just found out no one likes her
jokes.punchline: Homer's assumption that Lisa was already aware of her unpopularity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - brutally honest and perfectly economical. Great character integration.

jokes.text: If gasoline is their food, then why do they have teeth?!
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining his fear of Cars characters
jokes.punchline: Logical inconsistency in animated car anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd child logic about Pixar Cars. Perfect for Milhouse's neurotic character.

jokes.text: Message boards are never wrong.
jokes.setup: Discussion of anonymous internet praise for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's faith in internet message board accuracy
jokes.explanation: Sharp internet satire delivered with perfect economy. Homer's naive faith is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Lisa, what you did is like my mom sticking ten bucks in her own G-string.
jokes.setup: Student criticizing Lisa for fake anonymous praise
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate comparison involving the student's mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird and specific analogy. The inappropriateness makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Truth Teller? More like Lie Smeller!
jokes.setup: Students turning on Lisa after revelation
jokes.punchline: Childish but effective rhyming insult
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyming insult that works for kids but isn't particularly clever. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: That's it, I am off of this stuff. Me, too, sweetheart. Kids? Yes, Mama. No exceptions!
jokes.setup: Appears to be about drugs or alcohol
jokes.punchline: Family giving up something innocuous (likely TV/media) together
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with family dynamic. Works as visual gag but modest impact.

jokes.text: the McKinley funeral cortege paused to pick up casket ice
jokes.setup: Comparing Lady Gaga's train to most culturally significant trains
jokes.punchline: Darkly specific detail about presidential funeral logistics
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific historical detail. The 'casket ice' is perfectly morbid.

jokes.text: I try not to cry till you're asleep.
jokes.setup: Marge comforting depressed Lisa
jokes.punchline: Marge's admission of scheduled emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark and economical. Reveals Marge's struggle with parenting Lisa in devastating way.

jokes.text: It's not a frown. It is a straight line of resignation. Looks just the same upside down. What are you talking about? Y-You're right!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to cheer Lisa by turning her frown upside down
jokes.punchline: Lisa's geometric description and Homer's realization both work visually
jokes.explanation: Clever visual and verbal comedy. Lisa's precise language contrasts with Homer's simple logic.

jokes.text: I will, but you know me and my crazy number thing where I always have to get to 17. Voop! Voop! Voop!
jokes.setup: Homer promising to stop trying to help
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with nonsensical compulsion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - cannot resist making things worse while trying to help. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Uh, actually, I'm half monster, half Armenian. Pick your poison.
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga calling audience 'Little Monsters'
jokes.punchline: Audience member's ethnic identity confusion and dark punchline
jokes.explanation: Good ethnic humor that plays on stereotypes without being mean-spirited. Solid structure.

jokes.text: Disco Stu-tiful. Square root-iful. Old coot-iful
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga's song about everyone being beautiful
jokes.punchline: Character-specific pun variations on 'beautiful'
jokes.explanation: Nice character-specific wordplay. Frink's 'square root-iful' is particularly clever.

jokes.text: Lie Smeller! Lie Smeller!
jokes.setup: Students continue chanting the insult
jokes.punchline: The insult has now become a persistent identifier
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that shows how school insults stick. Not particularly creative but serves story.

jokes.text: That kind of thing sounds hollow from anyone but you!
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga asking if everyone loves themselves
jokes.punchline: Audience member's cynical but accurate assessment of celebrity inspiration
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on celebrity culture and inspirational messages. Good economy.

jokes.text: It's one of those music industry superstars who's turning our innocent children into ladies of the night. What sweater drawer did you crawl out of?
jokes.setup: Ned's moral objection to Lady Gaga
jokes.punchline: Gaga's dismissive comeback about Ned's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character clash. Gaga's comeback is specific and visual, playing off Ned's conservative appearance.

jokes.text: Darn it, the Bible said the Devil would be attractive. What makes you think I'm the Devil?
jokes.setup: Ned's theological concern about attractive evil
jokes.punchline: Gaga's logical trap making Ned admit she's attractive
jokes.explanation: Clever logical trap that uses Ned's religious framework against him. Well-structured reversal.

jokes.text: Unless you're gonna slap some wieners on those, turn 'em off.
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga's elaborate costume/performance
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical food-focused demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - reduces artistic expression to food practicality. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Joke's on you; I needed a trim.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently ruining her costume/hair
jokes.punchline: Gaga turning potential disaster into planned grooming
jokes.explanation: Good recovery line that shows Gaga's adaptability. Nice economy and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: You idiots! She meant we should disperse.
jokes.setup: Flash mob continuing after Lady Gaga leaves
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pedantic correction about misunderstood instruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - pedantically correcting others' social cues. Good character work.

jokes.text: I wish I was one of those young women who could just wave their hands in front of their eyes and not cry, because it doesn't work for me.
jokes.setup: Marge struggling with how to help Lisa
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to failed emotional control technique
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about failed self-help techniques. Good character vulnerability from Marge.

jokes.text: This is like the night Hillary won the New Hampshire Primary.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific political comparison to romantic intimacy
jokes.explanation: Weird and specific political reference that deflates romantic moment. Typical Homer non sequitur.

jokes.text: Some of that meat is me. Can I just trim the hem?
jokes.setup: Homer wearing meat dress
jokes.punchline: Treating his own flesh as fabric to be tailored
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual gag with Homer's practical approach to avant-garde fashion

jokes.text: Her eyes shoot loser lasers!
jokes.setup: Students running from Lisa
jokes.punchline: Supernatural explanation for Lisa's social toxicity
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and economical. 'Loser lasers' is a perfect kid-logic explanation.

jokes.text: Are you crying... glitter? Tiny diamonds. Hurts like hell.
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga appearing to cry glitter
jokes.punchline: Escalation to diamonds and physical pain
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from glitter to diamonds with painful consequence. Perfect Gaga characterization.

jokes.text: I cheat on my diet, but nobody knows 'cause the damage is on the inside, and the first warning sign is sudden death.
jokes.setup: Homer giving parenting advice about cheating
jokes.punchline: Morbid revelation about his diet deception consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Homer logic. The escalation to 'sudden death' is perfectly absurd and in-character.

jokes.text: I cheat on my diet, but nobody knows 'cause the damage is on the inside, and the first warning sign is sudden death.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how to cheat without getting caught
jokes.punchline: His casual acceptance of potentially fatal health consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer humor - treating fatal health consequences as minor inconvenience. The logic is twisted but consistent.

jokes.text: Parenting. It's the one job you know you're always doing right.
jokes.setup: Homer's completely wrong parenting advice to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional confidence in his parenting skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic statement from Homer. The confidence is inversely related to competence. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Why is she doing in defeat what I do in victory?
jokes.setup: Lady Gaga lying in bed eating Fruit Roll-Ups
jokes.punchline: Homer's recognition of his own lazy celebration habits
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness from Homer. The observation about matching behavior for opposite reasons is sharp.

jokes.text: On a one-way train to be Milhouse's bride
jokes.setup: Lisa's song about her low self-worth
jokes.punchline: Ultimate nightmare scenario of marrying Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the series' running gag. Milhouse as ultimate fate is perfectly Lisa's nightmare.

jokes.text: Can you help me with my self-esteem? I'm not that good.
jokes.setup: Ralph asking for confidence help
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating the exact problem he wants help with
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - undermines his own request immediately. Great economy showing the circular nature of low self-esteem.

jokes.text: I was one of Gaga's dancers. The one with the spangliest crotch.
jokes.setup: Narrator revealing involvement in the story
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate detail about costume
jokes.explanation: Funny ending reveal with appropriately inappropriate detail. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: I love my Homer face. Yeah, that's what I said. Right where it should be on the front of my head.
jokes.setup: Homer singing about loving his face
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious location of his face as if it's noteworthy
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer absurdist logic about basic anatomy. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

tmdb_overview: Lisa tries to make herself more popular by writing good things about herself on the school blog, but the plan backfires; Lisa helps Lady Gaga and learns the importance of being yourself.
